summary: The downstream trie.rs divergence is deterministic rustfmt noise only: the StateRootError-to-DatabaseError match arm was reflowed from a trailing-pipe pattern to rustfmt's leading-pipe pattern with no branch, conversion, constant, encoding, or negative-path semantic change.
notes: Queue entry verified at line 103 as `M	crates/evm/execution-errors/src/trie.rs` before writing.

Source patch extracted only for crates/evm/execution-errors/src/trie.rs from ritual-reth-internal-downstream-from-divergence.diff; the next diff header begins immediately after the single rustfmt hunk.

The only source hunk changes `StateRootError::Database(err) | StateRootError::StorageRootError(StorageRootError::Database(err)) => err` formatting to the rustfmt leading-pipe style; the matched variants and returned database error are unchanged.

Exact-code-first destination search found no exact `impl From<StateRootError> for DatabaseError` copy in the destination repos. The v2.2 port contains the same-path trie.rs with an upstream-shaped `impl From<StateRootError> for ProviderError` and an analogous database/storage database error arm, but this noise record does not rely on structural equivalence because there are no behavior-bearing atoms to map.
No execution error, trie validation, or negative-path semantic atom was identified; high-risk accounting is therefore zero.
No index, review queue, validation summary, progress, NEXT_ACTION, or implementation files were modified.
